data.narrative:
Infleqtion, a publicly traded quantum computing company founded by Dana Anderson and Pranav Gokhale, is evaluated against a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage companies. The quantum computing public market includes IonQ ($11.0B), Rigetti Computing ($5.5B), D-Wave Quantum ($2.0B), and Archer Materials ($60M), spanning a valuation range of $60M to $11.0B.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields an implied valuation of $3.8B. This represents the median across the four public quantum computing comparables. Infleqtion's current valuation of $1.8B sits materially below this peer median, positioning it in the lower quartile of the public quantum computing cohort despite having raised $210M in total capital.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $3.2B (low) to $4.3B (high), centered on the $3.8B peer median. The wide dispersion in peer valuations—from $60M to $11.0B—reflects significant uncertainty in how the market prices quantum computing companies at this stage. Key uncertainties include the reliability of peer comparables given the nascent nature of the sector, potential differences in technology maturity across competitors, and revenue visibility. A single method triangulation limits conviction; additional revenue multiple or discounted cash flow analysis would strengthen the valuation framework.
